note: Two linked systems, content-defined, needing light engine support (flagged): (1) THE GAME — a formal ladder of skill-contests played for stakes, the diegetic way to earn coin, standing, favors, and even recruits; inspired by Stile's Game in the Blue Adept / Apprentice Adept series. (2) COIN — the money system underneath it and the whole economy. The Game is HOW a moneyless newcomer climbs; coin is WHAT they climb with. This resolves Erik's money-system and recruit-for-pay asks together. Engine work needed is minimal and flagged in buildNeeds; the CONTENT (currencies, the Game's structure, contest types, stakes) is authored here. Authored by Aevi (PO), from Erik's Blue Adept framing.

coin.principle: Wealth is real, tracked, and local. No single currency rules the whole valley — different powers mint different trust. This keeps money textured and makes MONEY-CHANGERS and FAVORS matter.

currencies.name: Chimes
currencies.form: small resonant discs that ring true or false
currencies.trust: high in the cities, discounted in the wilds
currencies.note: The nearest thing to a standard; a false chime rings wrong, so counterfeiting is hard.

currencies.name: Lumens
currencies.form: glass chits that hold a faint stored light
currencies.trust: high on the Plateau, traded elsewhere at a rate
currencies.note: A dead lumen (light gone out) is worthless; they must be kept 'lit,' which is its own small tax.

currencies.name: Toll-Chits
currencies.trust: law at the Roost, barter elsewhere
currencies.note: Already in the item kit — the Roost's favor-currency; spendable for passage, silence, doors.

currencies.name: Trade-Goods & Barter
currencies.note: The Stilts, Millbrook, the Wend, the wilds run largely on barter; coin is a city habit.

coin.earning: Work, quests, trade, salvage, reward — and, prominently, THE GAME. Spending: goods, passage, bribes, hiring, information, training under masters, upkeep (lit lumens, fed companions).
coin.buildNeeds_engine: GM-EYES: character gains a wallet {currency:amount} + a wealthLevel; prices are location+scarcity flavored (ties to affinities); the GM narrates costs, the engine tracks the ledger. Minimal: a wallet field, add/subtract on transactions, a wealthLevel derivation. Flagged for the money-system spec.

the_game.principle: Anywhere people gather at scale — the cities, the Roost, the bigger settlements — there is the Game: a formal, honored ladder of skill-contests for stakes. A newcomer with no coin and no name can walk in and, by winning, climb. The Game is the valley's meritocratic underbelly and its great equalizer — Stile's ladder, made local. It is how the powerless get purchase, and how the powerful recruit and test.
the_game.howItWorks: A contest is agreed between two parties: the STAKE (coin, a favor, an object, standing, service) and the FRAME (what kind of contest). The challenged party often gets to shape the frame; skilled players negotiate toward their strengths. Contests resolve through the normal engine (opposed checks / staged challenges), flavored by frame. Win, take the stake and a rung of standing; lose, pay it.

contestFrames.name: Contests of Wit
contestFrames.flavor: riddle-duels, strategy-games, debates, puzzles — the Cogitant's arena

contestFrames.name: Contests of Body
contestFrames.flavor: races, climbs, feats of skill and nerve — not to the death, to the yield

contestFrames.name: Contests of Craft
contestFrames.flavor: make the better thing, fix the unfixable, out-tinker the rival — Orrin's home ground

contestFrames.name: Contests of Voice
contestFrames.flavor: persuade a crowd, out-sing, out-bargain, win the room — the Wend and the mediators shine

contestFrames.name: Contests of Power
contestFrames.flavor: a formal, non-lethal test of the crafts themselves — resonance against resonance, will against will; the high-stakes frame, and the one powers watch

contestFrames.name: Contests of Chance
contestFrames.flavor: the honest gamble — dice, draws, wagers; where Wildcraft and a good rune-read tilt the odds without breaking them

stakes: standing (a rung on the local Game-ladder)
SERVICE — the loser travels with / works for the winner for a term (this is one route to recruits)
the_game.standing: Winning builds Game-standing, a local reputation that opens doors, better stakes, and eventually invitations from powers who recruit through the Game. A high Game-standing is its own kind of wealth and its own kind of target.
the_game.recruitmentTie: The Game is a primary RECRUIT path: win someone's service as a stake; be recruited by a power impressed by your climb; meet future companions across the contest-floor. Ties directly to the recruit-for-pay-or-purpose ask.
the_game.buildNeeds_engine: GM-EYES: mostly resolves through existing engines (opposed checks, staged challenges, clamped ability-contests). New: a Game-standing track per hub, stake-resolution (transfer coin/favor/service on win-loss), and a contest-framing UI affordance. Flagged for the money+recruitment spec; the Game is where recruitment and money share machinery.

recruitment.principle: NPCs can join the party for PAY or for JOINT PURPOSE — both are real, and they behave differently. A paid companion serves while the coin and respect last; a purpose-joined companion stays while the cause holds, and a bonded one stays past both. Money buys presence; purpose buys loyalty; bond buys the thing money can't.
routes: hire for wages (needs coin; leaves if unpaid or ill-used)
win service as a Game stake (a term of service, honored by Game-law)
recruit to a shared cause (an NPC whose want aligns with the player's arc joins for the purpose, not the pay)
earn a bond (a companion who has crossed enough with you stays for you — the existing bond system)
recruitment.loyaltyModel: A recruit's staying-power = why they came. Paid: loyalty tracks pay + treatment + a better offer elsewhere. Purpose: loyalty tracks whether the cause still holds and the player still serves it. Bonded: loyalty is the bond band. A paid recruit can BECOME purpose-joined can become bonded — that arc is the point.
recruitment.buildNeeds_engine: GM-EYES: recruit as a party-member with a joinReason {pay|purpose|bond}, a wage or a cause reference, and a loyalty check on stress. Reuses the companion/bond machinery + the new wallet. Flagged for the spec.

buildNeeds_summary: Content here is authorable-now and mostly done. Engine needs, to spec as one batch (money+Game+recruitment share machinery): a wallet + wealthLevel; location-flavored prices; a Game-standing track + stake-transfer resolution; recruit-as-party-member with joinReason + loyalty. All reuse existing check/challenge/bond engines — this is a medium spec, not a rebuild. Aevi to write it when Erik greenlights turning from content to systems.
